- "Microsoft Power Manager Version 1.0"
- "OFF"
- "C:\WINDOWS\SMARTDRV.EXE 2048 128"
- "PROMP" (probably PROMPT was partially shown)

. But there's also "OFF" which could be from the power manager. The text shows things are processing. Let me wait longer and check again.

But wait - the line at row 11 shows the echo of the SMARTDRV command, and row 12 shows "PROMP" which is part of "PROMPT $p$g". This means autoexec.bat is still being processed. Let me wait more.

Also interesting - the previous boot was hanging because of `C:\DOSIDLE.EXE`, `C:\WINDOWS\net start`, and `fdapm poweroff`. I removed those. Now the boot should proceed to just SMARTDRV,

then PROMPT, PATH, SET TEMP, then `win`.

Let me wait another 30 seconds and check again.
